_comment: chrome.storage.local fixture for testing the PromptMate rating prompt banner. This data lives in Extension Storage (chrome.storage.local), NOT in the page's localStorage.
storage_location: chrome.storage.local — visible in Chrome DevTools → Application tab → Extension Storage → Local

seen_no_action._description: Banner was shown at least once (firstSeenAt set) but user has not rated or dismissed. Use this state to reliably surface the 'A QUICK FAVOR' banner on next panel open.

eligible_unseen._description: Banner has never been shown. Extension will show it once its usage threshold is met. Less predictable for testing — prefer seen_no_action.

dismissed._description: User clicked X on the banner. Banner should not reappear immediately.

rated._description: User clicked Rate PromptMate. Banner should not reappear.

method_url_params._description: The pm_test_key/pm_test_value hook is implemented in scripts/test-seam.js (build-gated; dev builds only). The content script reads these params on load, validates the key against ALLOWED_TEST_KEYS, writes the value into chrome.storage.local, strips the params, and reloads once. No DevTools or console access required — any agent that can navigate a URL can use this.
method_url_params.reusable_for_other_keys: The same pm_test_key / pm_test_value params work for any chrome.storage.local key, not just promptmate.ratingPrompt. Use the same pattern to seed onboarding state, feature flags, or any other storage-backed banner.

method_application_tab._description: Manual fallback — works today without any product changes. Not usable by automated agents because Chrome automation cannot open DevTools panels.
steps: Open Chrome DevTools on the supported platform page (F12 or right-click → Inspect)
In the left sidebar expand 'Extension Storage' then 'Local'
Find the PromptMate extension entry and click it
Double-click the value cell and replace it with the desired state JSON from this file
Open PromptMate panel — the banner should appear at the top
